title: Extract the reusable rules-explainer engine (schema + readability/a11y/i18n pipeline + not-advice layer + change-watch + eval) for adjacent domains
context: Benefits-Navigator is an openly-licensed corpus of plain-language, primary-source-cited, multilingual, accessible guides to public-assistance programs (SNAP, Medicaid/CHIP, WIC, TANF, LIHEAP, SSI, housing, EITC, Lifeline/ACP), delivered as a static site, printable PDFs, and structured data with an optional retrieval-grounded explainer. It is a HIGH risk-tier project: it provides information, not legal/benefits advice, never makes an individualized eligibility determination, and always routes people to the official application and free human help. The PLAN.md v0.2 Adjacent Opportunities section identifies that the defining constraint — a reusable, openly-licensed rules-explainer with currency-tracking — generalizes beyond benefits to any high-stakes, frequently-changing, jurisdiction-varying rule corpus (immigration procedure, tax, housing code, disability/veterans benefits, know-your-rights). The benefits corpus is the first instantiation; the engine (claim-to-source schema, readability/a11y/i18n pipeline, not-advice/refusal policy layer, staleness fail-safe, change-watch, grounded-vs-blank-slate eval harness) is the durable, reusable asset. This task produces the design spec and extraction plan for that engine — a post-delivery artifact gated on a stable benefits corpus. No partner, credentialed reviewer, or requestor is yet secured.
objective: Produce a design specification and extraction plan for the reusable rules-explainer engine underlying Benefits-Navigator — defining the abstraction boundary between the benefits corpus and the engine components, the extension points for adjacent sibling projects, which packages are candidates for extraction, and the governance constraints that any instantiation must honor.
acceptanceCriteria: Defines the abstraction boundary between the benefits-navigator corpus (jurisdiction-specific, program-specific data and reviewer sign-offs) and the reusable engine components: the claim-to-source schema with effectiveDate/pendingChange/supersededBy, the readability/a11y/i18n pipeline, the not-advice/refusal policy layer, the staleness fail-safe, the monitored-sources change-watch, and the grounded-vs-blank-slate eval harness

Specifies concrete extension points and how a sibling project (know-your-rights, financial-literacy-open, community-resource-maps) would instantiate the engine with a different corpus and domain-specific rules — without duplicating the safety or currency machinery

Identifies which packages are stable enough to extract (packages/schema, packages/provenance, lib/policy, lib/content, packages/redteam, packages/watch) and which remain benefits-specific; defines the extraction sequence and backward-compatibility constraints

Includes a governance note confirming the engine stays MIT-licensed and that the not-advice guardrails, route-to-human requirement, and zero-PII constraint are mandatory for any instantiation involving high-stakes content (benefits, immigration, legal rights, tax, housing) — the moat that makes the corpus safe to consult and embed
